comments.author: example-reader
comments.text: The second-order problem is the hardest part — the expert watches the newcomer hit a gap and still doesn't close it. Naming that explicitly is unusual; most 'documentation' advice stops at 'write it down.'

comments.author: example-reader
comments.text: 'Fish-in-the-water in linguistic compression' — the *just* as the diagnostic word. I'm going to notice this in my own speech now.

comments.author: example-reader
comments.text: 'A surface that unstated assumptions can reflect off of' — rephrasing the agent's lack-of-intuition as a reflective surface rather than a deficit is the quiet reframe this essay earns.
